pattern: In a sorted array the element that appears more than 25 percent of the time must sit at one of the quarter-length boundary positions; check each candidate with binary search for O(log n) total cost.
hints: The dominant element must cover a contiguous block whose length exceeds n/4; in a sorted array that means it touches at least one of the positions n/4, n/2, or 3n/4.
For each candidate at those quarter positions, binary-search for its first and last occurrence and check whether the span is more than n/4.
Alternatively, a single linear pass counting runs suffices; or compare arr[i] with arr[i + n//4] — if equal, that value is the answer.

edge_inputs.description: entire array is the same value
length-1 array, the only element wins trivially
length-2 array, both entries are the same
dominant element sits at the far right
dominant element sits at the far left
exactly one more than 25 percent, not a tie

stress.description: large sorted array with a planted dominant value
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(4, size)
    dom = rng.randint(0, 100000)
    dom_count = n // 4 + 1
    others = sorted(rng.randint(0, 100000) for _ in range(n - dom_count))
    # replace any clashes
    arr = sorted(others + [dom] * dom_count)
    return [arr]
